### Post by wulfhound on 2008-10-21
Here is my xorg:

```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Configured Video Device"
	Driver		"nvidia"
	Option		"NoLogo"	"True"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Configured Monitor"
        ModelName      "Mitsubishi NXM76LCD"
        HorizSync       31.0 - 69.0
        VertRefresh     56.0 - 75.0
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Monitor		"Configured Monitor"
	Device		"Configured Video Device"
	Defaultdepth	24
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
  screen "Default Screen"
EndSection
Section "Module"
	Load		"glx"
EndSection
```

I have just installed Ubuntu a few days ago, and I'm using the nvidia driver.

This is the card I have: [http://www.newegg.com/Product/Product.aspx?Item=N82E16814127329](http://www.newegg.com/Product/Product.aspx?Item=N82E16814127329)

It's a MSI NX8800GT 512M OC GeForce 8800 GT 512MB 256-bit GDDR3 PCI Express 2.0 x16 HDCP Ready SLI Supported Video Card

My monitor is a Mitsubishi DiamondPoint NXM76LCD. The HorizSync is 31.0-70, the VertRefresh 56.0-75.0...what I really want are these two items which I've found in another person's xorg using google, but I can't get to work.

When I add them, my xorg fails, and I try to choose it manually through the config. So I put it back to what is is above, without the modelines, and I can get all resolutions, except I can't get any good refresh rates.

At 1024x768, I can only pick from 56, 57, and 58 refresh rate. 50 and 51 at other larger resolutions.

I am willing to do whatever work it takes to fix this - but I really do need some help. I remember trying to use Ubuntu in the past and this was my hurdle to jump last time, and I failed. :(



 ModeLine       "1024x768@70" 75.0 1024 1048 1184 1328 768 771 777 806 -hsync -vsync
    ModeLine       "1280x1024@60" 108.0 1280 1328 1440 1688 1024 1025 1028 1066 +hsync +vsync
